title: 10 best herbal teas for oily face
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily face are infusions made from plants and herbs that are beneficial for reducing excess oil and balancing skin. They contain various compounds such as tannins, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory agents that help regulate sebum production, soothe irritation, and promote clearer skin. Examples of herbal teas that help with oily face include chamomile, green tea, and spearmint tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation, while green tea is rich in antioxidants that help regulate sebum production and protect the skin from damage. Spearmint tea, on the other hand, has been shown to reduce androgen levels, which can help reduce excess oil production. By incorporating these herbal teas into their skincare routine, individuals with oily face can experience improved skin texture, reduced shine, and a more balanced complexion, leading to increased confidence and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with oily face because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation in the skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy skin cell function. Additionally, lemon balm has antibacterial properties that can help prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin. Drinking lemon balm tea can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances that lead to oily skin. By incorporating lemon balm tea into your daily routine, you can help maintain a clearer, more balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid and other antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the production of excess oil.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea, such as eugenol and Ursolic acid, help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin and acne.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea has antimicrobial properties due to its content of citral and other compounds, which can help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin.
Astringent properties: The tannins present in lemon balm tea have astringent properties that help tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oily skin and preventing the buildup of dirt and bacteria.

Calming properties: Lemon balm tea contains compounds such as rosmarinic acid and citral that have calming and soothing effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can contribute to the production of excess oil in the skin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Optionally, add 1 teaspoon of honey for added benefits and taste.
Cleanse your face with a gentle cleanser, then use a cotton ball to apply the tea to your face.
Leave the tea on your face for 10-15 minutes, then rinse off with lukewarm water and pat dry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with oily face because it contains menthol and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate oil production and soothe irritated skin. The menthol in peppermint tea has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help reduce inflammation and redness. Additionally, peppermint tea has antibacterial properties that can help prevent acne and other skin issues that are often associated with oily skin. Drinking peppermint tea can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to excess oil production. By incorporating peppermint tea into your daily routine, you can help maintain healthy, balanced skin and reduce the appearance of an oily face.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Peppermint tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent bacteria growth on the skin, important for oily face as excess oil can lead to bacterial growth and acne. The active constituents responsible for this property are menthol and menthone.

Astringent: Peppermint tea has astringent properties that help reduce excess oil production and tighten pores, important for oily face as it helps maintain a matte finish and reduces the likelihood of acne. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, important for oily face as it helps soothe irritated skin. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant: Peppermint tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, important for oily face as it helps maintain a healthy and youthful appearance. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Peppermint tea has circulation-boosting properties that help increase blood flow to the skin, important for oily face as it helps promote skin cell turnover and regeneration. The active constituents responsible for this property are menthol and menthone.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves to the boiling water, then steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the peppermint leaves, discarding the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Apply the tea to your face using a cotton ball, leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing off.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with oily face because it has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce the appearance of large pores. Thyme is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote skin cell regeneration. Drinking thyme tea can help detoxify the body and eliminate toxins that can contribute to oily skin. Additionally, thyme tea can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief for those who experience acne or other skin conditions associated with oily skin. Overall, thyme tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage oily skin and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria, important for oily face as it can help prevent breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.

Astringent: Thyme tea has astringent properties that help to reduce excess oil and tighten pores, important for oily face as it helps to regulate sebum production and improve skin texture. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, important for oily face as it can help soothe irritated skin and reduce the appearance of acne. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, important for oily face as it can help to prevent premature aging and improve overall skin health. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and luteolin.

Circulation-boosting: Thyme tea has circulation-boosting properties that help to increase blood flow to the skin, important for oily face as it can help to nourish the skin and promote the healing of blemishes. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the tea steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves, pouring the liquid into a clean container.
Allow the thyme tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Use a cotton ball to apply the cooled thyme tea to your oily face, leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing off.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with oily face because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can regulate oil production in the skin. The tannins present in sage can help to tighten pores, preventing excess sebum from accumulating. Additionally, sage has antibacterial properties that can help to reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria. Drinking sage tea can also help to detoxify the body, which can lead to clearer and more balanced skin. Regular consumption of sage tea can help to control oil production, reduce the appearance of pores, and promote a clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Sage tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent acne and other skin infections caused by bacteria, which is important for oily face as it is more prone to such issues due to excess sebum production. The active constituents responsible for this property include camphor, camphene, and salvene.
Astringent: Sage tea has astringent properties that help control excess oil production and tighten pores, preventing clogging and blackheads. This is achieved through the tannins present in sage.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne and other skin conditions. This is attributed to the rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids found in sage.

Antioxidant: Sage tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from free radical damage, promoting cell regeneration and healing. This property is important for oily face as it aids in maintaining a healthy and youthful appearance. The primary antioxidants in sage are rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and carnosol.

Antibacterial: Sage tea exhibits antibacterial properties that help eliminate acne-causing bacteria and prevent further breakouts. This property is essential for oily face as it inhibits the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium responsible for acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include thujone, camphor, and 1,8-cineole.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves to the hot water.
Steep the sage leaves in hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down and use it as a facial toner, twice a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tea helps with oily face because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants and essential oils, such as terpinen-4-ol and γ-terpinene, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent acne. Moreover, marjoram tea has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and give the skin a matte finish. Drinking marjoram tea can also help detoxify the body and eliminate toxins that can cause skin problems. Regular consumption of marjoram tea can help balance the skin's oil production, reduce shine, and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Marjoram tea has antiseptic properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, important for oily face as excess oil can create a breeding ground for bacteria that cause acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.

Astringent: Marjoram tea has astringent properties that help to shrink and tighten the skin pores, reducing the amount of oil that the skin produces. This property is important for oily face as enlarged pores can lead to an increase in oil production and acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling of the skin, important for oily face as inflammation can worsen acne and lead to scarring. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpinene-4-ol and γ-terpinene.

Antioxidant: Marjoram tea has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, important for oily face as oxidative stress can exacerbate acne and lead to premature aging. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Circulation-boosting: Marjoram tea has circulation-boosting properties that help to improve blood flow to the skin, important for oily face as increased blood flow can help to reduce the production of excess oil and improve the overall health of the skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include β-caryophyllene and α-humulene.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 2 cups of water and let it cool down to 205°F-212°F (96°C-100°C) for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried marjoram leaves to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the marjoram leaves and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the marjoram leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before gently applying it to your face using a cotton ball. Let it sit for 10-15 minutes, then rinse off with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with oily face because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, rosemary contains carnosic acid, which has been shown to help regulate skin oil production, making it an effective natural remedy for oily skin. The tea also has antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature aging. Furthermore, rosemary tea has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and give the skin a more matte appearance. Drinking rosemary tea or using it as a toner can provide a natural and gentle way to manage oily skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Rosemary tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent bacteria from accumulating in the pores, which is important for oily face as excess oil can lead to bacterial growth and acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and essential oils such as cineol, camphene, and borneol.

Astringent: The astringent properties of rosemary tea help tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of large pores and controlling excess oil production. This is achieved through the tannins present in rosemary, which have a contracting effect on skin proteins.

Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, which is beneficial for oily face as inflammation can exacerbate oil production and lead to skin breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, carnosol, and carnosic acid.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tea contains potent antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors. This is important for oily face as antioxidants can help prevent premature aging and promote overall skin health. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, carnosol, and carnosic acid.

Circulation-boosting: Rosemary tea can help improve blood circulation, which promotes the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the skin. This is beneficial for oily face as improved circulation can help regulate oil production and promote a healthy, radiant complexion. The active constituents responsible for this property include ursolic acid and essential oils such as cineol, camphene, and borneol.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves to the hot water.
Steep the rosemary leaves for 5-7 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the rosemary leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a facial toner, twice a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with oily face because it contains antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the skin. The tea's antioxidants can also help to unclog pores, reducing the likelihood of acne and other skin blemishes. Additionally, lavender has been shown to have calming effects on the skin, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin. Drinking lavender tea can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to the production of excess oil in the skin. Overall, incorporating English lavender tea into your skincare routine can help to balance oil production, reduce inflammation, and promote healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: English lavender tea has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria responsible for causing acne, which is important for oily face as it can help prevent breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, eucalyptol, and camphor.

Sebum regulation: English lavender tea helps regulate sebum production, which is important for oily face as it can help reduce excess oiliness and shine. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalyl acetate and ursolic acid.

Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne, which is important for oily face as it can help soothe and calm irritated skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and caryophyllene.

Antioxidant: English lavender tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for oily face as it can help prevent premature aging and promote healthy-looking skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and apigenin.

Soothing: English lavender tea has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which is important for oily face as it can help reduce irritation and inflammation caused by excess oiliness and acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and lavandulol.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the lavender tea to remove the flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Cleanse your face with a gentle cleanser.
Use a cotton ball to apply the cooled lavender tea to your face, avoiding the eye area. Let it air dry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with oily face because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin. The tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which are antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, pot marigold tea has anti-bacterial properties that can help prevent acne and other skin infections that are often associated with oily skin. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a toner or compress can help reduce excess oil, unclog pores, and promote clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, common in oily face, due to its high content of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antibacterial: Its antibacterial properties help fight against the bacteria that can cause acne, making it an effective remedy for oily face, thanks to the presence of essential oils and alkaloids.
Astringent: Pot marigold tea acts as an astringent, helping to tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, due to the tannins present in the plant.
Soothing: Its soothing properties help to calm and reduce irritation, ideal for oily and sensitive skin, because of the flavonoids and saponins found in the plant.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of pot marigold tea helps protect skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier-looking skin, due to the presence of carotenoids and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried marigold flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the herb to release its oils and active compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the marigold flowers, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey for added antioxidants and a touch of sweetness, if desired.
Drink the tea twice a day, once in the morning and once in the evening, for best results in managing oily skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with oily face because it has natural detoxifying properties that can help regulate sebum production in the skin. This is due to its high levels of vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, which can help reduce inflammation and improve skin health. Additionally, dandelion tea may help improve liver function, which can also contribute to healthy skin. By reducing the production of excess oil, dandelion tea can help prevent acne breakouts and promote clearer, more balanced skin. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Overall, drinking dandelion tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage oily skin and improve skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin irritations that often accompany oily skin.

Sebum regulation: Dandelion tea is known to help regulate sebum production due to its richness in chicoric and chlorogenic acids, which can help maintain the skin's natural oil balance and reduce excessive oiliness.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of dandelion tea, mainly due to its phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help combat the growth of acne-causing bacteria, thus preventing further breakouts and infections on oily skin.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, which can protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the signs of aging that can be associated with oily skin.

Detoxification: Dandelion tea supports the body's natural detoxification processes, aiding in the elimination of toxins that can contribute to skin issues. This is partly due to its high content of taraxacin, taraxacerin, and other bitter glycosides, which can help promote liver health and clearer skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Rinse the dandelion parts under cold running water, then let them air dry on a paper towel.
Crush or chop the dandelion flowers and leaves, placing them in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dandelion pieces, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.

Remove the tea infuser or strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the spent dandelion material. Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature before consuming. Enjoy daily for best results in maintaining a balanced skin complexion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with oily face because it has astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the yarrow plant, which contains tannins that can help tighten pores and provide a matte finish to the skin. Additionally, yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, providing a more balanced and clear complexion. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a toner can help maintain the skin's natural oil balance, preventing excess oil production and promoting a healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tea has an astringent property that helps to reduce excess oil production, tighten pores, and prevent acne, due to the tannins present in the plant.
Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory property soothes irritated skin and reduces redness, as it contains flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones that help alleviate inflammation.
Antibacterial: Yarrow tea's antibacterial property helps to eliminate acne-causing bacteria and prevent further breakouts, thanks to the presence of alpha-pinene and sabinene.
Balancing sebum production: Yarrow tea helps regulate sebum production, promoting a balanced skin condition, because of the azulene and salicylic acid content.

Soothing: Yarrow tea's soothing property calms and relieves skin irritation and inflammation, contributing to an overall healthier and more balanced complexion, due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers, simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Turn off heat, cover and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove yarrow flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea once or twice a day, consistently for best results in managing oily skin.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily face with herbal teas is by using them as toners. Brew a strong tea from herbs known to control oil production, such as green tea, chamomile, or sage. Allow the tea to cool, then apply it to your face with a cotton ball after cleansing. The tea's tannins will help to remove excess oil and tighten pores. For best results, use twice daily, and store the tea in the refrigerator to extend its shelf life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily face that are treated with herbal teas include acne and skin inflammation. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Similarly, spearmint tea can help regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to oily skin and breakouts. Additionally, green tea, which is rich in antioxidants, can help reduce inflammation and improve skin complexion. It is important to note that while herbal teas can be beneficial, they should not replace medical treatment, especially for severe cases of acne or other skin conditions. As with any treatment, it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new regimen.
